questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?

Which of the following best describes the first issue with the code to the right? That is, which error, if any, is both accurate in its description and will cause the code to break at the earliest point possible?

choices.A: line 1 will cause a compilation error.
choices.B: line 2 will cause a compilation error.
choices.C: line 2 will cause a runtime error.
choices.D: line 3 will cause a runtime error.
choices.E: line 4 will cause a compilation error.
choices.F: line 4 will cause a runtime error.

questions.explanation: C All statements will compile just fine. The first statement to cause an error will be line 2 since it attempts to allocate negative space, which is not allowed. Lines 3 and 4 will both cause runtime errors, but since the question asks for the first error, the correct answer is C.
questions.code:
int[] a = new int[] {}; // line 1
int[] b = new int[-1];  // line 2
a[0] = 1;               // line 3
b[-1] = 1;              // line 4

questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: B a[i]++ uses a[0]=2 as the target index (then makes a[0]=3), the RHS becomes a[1]+a[1]=0, and since b aliases a it assigns a[2]=0, leaving 3 0 0 0 2 → 30002.

questions.stem: What is output by the line marked //q10 in the code to the right?

choices.E: There is no output due to a runtime error.

questions.explanation: D Matrix Tracing.

questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: B First, b = a makes b an alias of a. The loop builds a difference array in c: c becomes {3, -2, 6, -5, 0}. Note c[4] stays 0 because the loop runs to a.length - 1 exclusive, so only indices 0–3 are written. Then b = c breaks b's alias to a and makes b point at c instead. Now b and c reference the same object, while a is independent. a[0] += b[0] + c[0]: since b and c are the same array, b[0] and c[0] are both 3. So a[0] = 1 + 3 + 3 = 7. b[0] = 0: this also sets c[0] to 0 since they're the same array. a is unaffected. Final state: a = {7, 4, 2, 8, 3}, b = c = {0, -2, 6, -5, 0}. String concatenation: "7" + "0" + "0" + "4" → "7004".
